Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Social media reaction to ‘in seine’ Olympics opening ceremony

The Paris Olympics opening ceremony was a lengthy and at times, simply crazy, event on Friday. Here is how social media reacted.

There is always huge interest in the opening ceremony at the Olympics, and there was certainlyplenty to talk aboutafter the torch was lit at the Paris Games on Friday night.

One of the most popular parts of the opening ceremony sees all participating countries have grand introductions, but the Paris Games already went a different route as the various Olympic teams were welcomed on boats cruising down theRiver Seinein a historic first for a Summer Games.

In between these introductions, there were various performances that ranged from dancing, parkour, singing to an outrageous runway fashion show. There was also a sensational performance from Celine Dion, torrential downpours that soaked the athletes, a song from a man bizarrely painted in blue, a torch handover process that seemed to last an age, and ultimately the entire opening ceremony lasted over four hours.

It was quite cleverly described as an opening ceremony that was simply ‘in seine’ as the use of the River Seine quite literally turned the event into a wild ride.

*What did you think of the Olympics opening ceremony? Let us know by leaving a comment at the end of this article
